content.problemStatement:
Write a program which does the following



Create a  `string list`  for the following values  `"Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", and "Thursday"`. 

Name the list as per your choice
Output the last two elements of the array on separate lines
content.outputFormat: Print the last two elements of the predefined list, each on its own line. The first line must be "Wednesday" and the second line must be "Thursday".

sampleTestCases.explanation: The predefined list is ["Monday","Tuesday","Wednesday","Thursday"]. The elements at positions 2 and 3 are "Wednesday" and "Thursday", printed on separate lines.
Even though there is no input, the program always prints the last two weekdays.
The solution is deterministic; the output does not depend on any external data.

solution.approach: The program creates a fixed array (or list) containing the four weekday strings. Since the list never changes, we directly access the elements at indices 2 and 3 (0‑based) which correspond to "Wednesday" and "Thursday". The solution loops over a single test case variable T (set to 1) to satisfy the requirement of handling T test cases, and prints the two strings on separate lines.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – only constant‑time array accesses and prints.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only the fixed array of four strings is stored.

code.Python3:
# No input is required for this problem.
week = ["Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday"]
T = 1  # single execution as there is no input
for _ in range(T):
    print(week[2])
    print(week[3])
